title: Agent Listing and Detail

story.i_want_to: list all registered agents and view individual agent details
story.so_that: I can see which agents are available, their types, their activity status, and when they were last seen

rationale: The orchestrator needs visibility into the agent pool to make intelligent assignment decisions and detect potential issues. Listing agents with their status and last_seen_at allows the orchestrator to route work to active implementers and avoid assigning to stale or deactivated agents. Filtering by agent_type lets the orchestrator quickly find just implementers or just other orchestrators. This is a read-only capability for orchestrator and higher roles.

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /api/v1/agents returns a paginated list of all agents in the tenant. Each entry includes id, name, agent_type, status, last_seen_at, metadata, inserted_at, and updated_at.
GET /api/v1/agents supports filtering by agent_type query parameter (e.g., ?agent_type=implementer). Returns only agents matching the specified type.
GET /api/v1/agents supports filtering by status query parameter (e.g., ?status=active). Returns only agents matching the specified status.
GET /api/v1/agents/:id returns the full agent detail including all fields. Returns 404 if the agent does not exist or belongs to a different tenant.
Both endpoints require orchestrator or user role. Agent-role API keys receive 403 Forbidden.
Tenant isolation is enforced: agents from other tenants are never visible. Cross-tenant GET requests return 404 for detail and empty list for index.
GET /api/v1/agents supports pagination via page and page_size query parameters. Default page_size is 25, maximum is 100.
The list endpoint returns agents sorted by name ascending by default. Supports sort_by query parameter with values: name, agent_type, status, last_seen_at, inserted_at.

test_cases.name: List all agents in tenant
expected_results: Each agent includes id, name, agent_type, status, last_seen_at, metadata
Agents sorted by name: orch-main, worker-1, worker-2

test_cases.name: Get agent detail
preconditions: fixture(:agent, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, name: "worker-1", agent_type: :implementer, status: :active, metadata: %{capabilities: ["elixir", "phoenix"]}})
expected_results: Includes all fields: id, name, agent_type, status, last_seen_at, metadata with capabilities, inserted_at, updated_at

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation: agents from other tenants not visible
steps: GET /api/v1/agents with tenant A orchestrator key
GET /api/v1/agents/:agent_b_id with tenant A orchestrator key
expected_results: Detail for agent_b returns 404 Not Found

test_cases.name: Pagination works correctly
expected_results: Response includes pagination metadata (total_count, page, page_size, total_pages)

technical_notes: Add list_agents/2 and get_agent/2 functions to the Loopctl.Agents context module.
list_agents/2 accepts a keyword list of filters: agent_type, status, page, page_size, sort_by.
Use Ecto.Query composable query pattern: start with base query, pipe through optional filter functions.
Pagination: use offset/limit with total_count in response metadata. Consider Scrivener or a simple custom implementation.
Controller at LoopctlWeb.AgentController with index and show actions.
Routes: GET /api/v1/agents and GET /api/v1/agents/:id. RequireRole plug set to [:orchestrator, :user, :superadmin].
RLS ensures tenant isolation at the database level. Application-level scoping by tenant_id is defense in depth.
All context functions MUST return `{:ok, result}` or `{:error, reason}` tuples. Never raise exceptions for expected error cases.
Controller MUST use `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController` for error rendering (maps error atoms to HTTP status codes per US-1.4)
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
